message.content: Adversaries may acquire capabilities through purchase, theft, or free downloads to enhance their operations. These capabilities include malware, software licenses, exploits, certificates, and vulnerability information. Rather than developing these tools in-house, adversaries often obtain them from technology companies, criminal marketplaces, or other adversaries. This acquisition supports various phases of their lifecycle, from initial access to execution and persistence. Common methods include buying from specialized vendors, downloading from the internet, or stealing from third-party entities. The impact of this technique can be significant, enabling adversaries to deploy sophisticated attacks without the need for internal development. Mitigation strategies include monitoring for unusual network traffic, implementing strict access controls, and maintaining up-to-date threat intelligence to identify and block known malicious tools and sources.
